script: When I first moved into my current house I started noticing that my trash cans were always slightly moved in the morning, like a few feet from where I left them. At first I thought it was just the wind or maybe the garbage collectors being careless. But then one night I happened to look outside around 3 AM and saw a raccoon standing on top of the lid like it was trying to break into a vault. The thing had clearly been doing this for weeks and had developed a whole routine: climb the side, jump on the lid, try to pry it open, fail, then drag the can a few inches while trying again. The funniest part was that it looked genuinely annoyed when the lid wouldn’t budge, like it was offended by my trash security. I eventually had to start using bungee cords to keep the lid shut, and for a few nights the raccoon would still come by, stare at the cords, and then just walk away like it was judging my life choices.
